title: Designing a Garden
rationale: Read a picture book and drew garden-design ideas; no hands-on cooking. The closing 'tasting' is a plain snack, not a structured sensory comparison, so no Tasting and no other skills.

title: Tastes Around the World
rationale: No hands-on cooking (foods are pre-prepped by the teacher), but students do a structured sensory gallery walk: they taste/smell foods from six country stations, record sensory observations on a graphic organizer, and compare using sensory language — a structured sensory tasting skill, so Tasting is credited.

title: Ancient China Bingo
rationale: A reading plus a bingo game about ancient China; no hands-on cooking. The closing 'tasting' of an ingredient is an eat-only snack, not a structured sensory activity, so the array is empty.

title: Food from the Eastern Hemisphere
rationale: Students prepare Chinese fried rice (cut veg → Knife skills; stir-fry eggs/carrots/rice in pans → Sautéing & stir-frying; graters → Grating; mixing bowls → Mixing & stirring) and sesame cauliflower (roasted on a baking sheet → Roasting; soy/maple/vinegar/cornstarch glaze → Creating sauces & dressings) from a recipe with measuring → Reading & following recipes, Measuring, Assembling. Rice is pre-cooked by the teacher, so the rice itself doesn't earn Boiling & simmering; tasting is an eat-only snack.

title: Fruit Salad Challenge
rationale: Teams build a fruit salad: cut fruit → Knife skills; mix in bowls → Mixing & stirring; optional honey/lemon/cinnamon dressing → Creating sauces & dressings; measuring spoons → Measuring; compose the salad → Assembling. Reading & following recipes is excluded because the lesson explicitly gives no recipe. The blind taste test is a favorite-picking preference vote, not a structured sensory skill, so no Tasting.

title: Go, Grow, Glow
rationale: Students build burrito bowls from a recipe: cook black beans in a pan on the cooktop → Boiling & simmering; chop/mince for the tomato-corn salsa → Knife skills, Mixing & stirring; portion ingredients → Measuring; compose the bowl → Assembling. The cilantro-lime rice is pre-cooked by the teacher, so the rice doesn't earn its own technique.

title: 5th Grade Food Cultures Unit Overview
rationale: A six-lesson unit overview where students prepare and taste a quick recipe each day at cooking tables with cutting boards/knives, following written recipes — entailing the core generic hands-on cooking skills of Knife skills, Measuring, and Reading & following recipes. Most country recipes are batch-cooked in advance, so no single specific technique is reliably student-performed across the unit; tastings are eat-only.

title: Chickpea Salad
rationale: Students chop celery/bell peppers and tear parsley → Knife skills; shake tahini/lemon/oil/salt into a dressing in a jar → Creating sauces & dressings; combine and mix with chickpeas → Mixing & stirring, Assembling; recipe is referenced → Reading & following recipes. Measuring is excluded because all dressing ingredients are pre-measured by the teacher. The thumbs vote is a preference vote, not structured Tasting.

title: Pico de Gallo (Knife Skills)
rationale: Knife-focused lesson: students chop tomato/onion/pepper/cilantro → Knife skills, combine with lime and salt → Mixing & stirring, Assembling, from a take-home recipe → Reading & following recipes. Kitchen & food safety is credited because the lesson delivers a dedicated knife-safety instructional segment (how to hold a knife, bear claw, saw) as taught content. The tried/liked/loved vote is a preference vote, not structured Tasting.

title: African American Foodways: Hoppin' John
rationale: Students dice peppers/celery/onions → Knife skills, cook Hoppin' John in a large pan on the stove (sauté aromatics then simmer with rice and black-eyed peas → Sautéing & stir-frying, Boiling & simmering), and make cornbread batter baked in the oven → Mixing & stirring, Baking. Recipe with measured ingredients → Measuring, Reading & following recipes; building the dish → Assembling. Peas are par-cooked in prep but finish cooking in the dish on the stove.

title: Vegetable Ramen
rationale: Students boil the noodles → Boiling & simmering; mince garlic, chop onion/mushrooms → Knife skills; grate ginger/carrot/cabbage → Grating; make furikake seasoning → Seasoning & spice blending; measure corn → Measuring; build the bowl with toppings → Assembling, from task/recipe cards → Reading & following recipes. The broth is pre-made by the teacher, so the broth itself isn't student-cooked, but the noodles are boiled in class.

title: Healthy Snacks (Energy Bites, Popcorn, Cracker Stack)
rationale: Three snack stations: roll pre-made dough and cracker-stacks → Assembling; season popcorn (and energy bites) with spice pinches and shake → Seasoning & spice blending; slice apples with salad knives → Knife skills; measuring spoons/cups → Measuring; laminated recipes and station directions → Reading & following recipes. Energy-bite dough is pre-made and popcorn is pre-popped by the teacher, so no Mixing or Boiling; the favorite-snack vote is not structured Tasting.

title: 3rd Grade Chinese-Style Dumplings
rationale: Students make and fill dumplings: cut scallions → Knife skills; mash cooked soybeans → Mashing; grate garlic/ginger/carrot → Grating; measure soy/oils/vinegar/white pepper and mix → Measuring, Mixing & stirring, Seasoning & spice blending; fill and pinch-seal the wonton wrappers → Wrapping & rolling, Assembling, from task cards → Reading & following recipes. Steaming is excluded because the teacher collects and steams all the dumplings; soybeans are pre-boiled in prep.

title: A Visit to Mashama Bailey’s Restaurant
rationale: Students cut collard greens with scissors → Knife skills, stew them in a pot on the burner → Boiling & simmering, and season greens (salt/sugar/red pepper/vinegar/oil) and grits (salt/pepper/cheese, stirring) → Seasoning & spice blending, Mixing & stirring, from task sheets → Reading & following recipes. Grits are pre-cooked and the onion pre-diced by the teacher, so no Boiling for the grits; the shared tasting is an eat-only snack.

title: Bees
rationale: A craft/garden lesson: students make a bee puppet, role-play pollination, and plant seeds; no hands-on cooking. The closing honey tasting is an eat-only snack, not a structured sensory activity, so the array is empty.

title: Recycle Gardening
rationale: A garden-only lesson: students plant seeds in recycled containers and discuss urban gardening; no cooking and no structured tasting, so the array is empty.

title: Seeds
rationale: A garden lesson with a seed dance and seed-saving; no hands-on cooking. The closing 'tasting' is an eat-only snack, not a structured sensory activity, so the array is empty.

title: edibleschoolyards_planting
rationale: A science/garden lesson: students observe and measure plant structures (in centimeters) and plant plants; no cooking and no structured food tasting, so the array is empty.

title: 2nd Grade Decomposition Experiment Part 1
rationale: A science lesson: students fill bags with soil and test items for a decomposition experiment and record predictions; no cooking. The closing 'tasting' is an eat-only snack, not a structured sensory activity, so the array is empty.

title: Gardening & The Environment
rationale: A garden-only lesson: students review and perform garden jobs (planting, weeding, harvesting, composting, watering); no cooking. The closing tasting of in-season fruit is an eat-only snack, so the array is empty.

title: The Garden in the Spring
rationale: A garden-only lesson: students hunt for signs of spring using laminated photos; no cooking and no structured tasting, so the array is empty.

title: September Sixth Grade Summer Plant Hunt
rationale: A garden lesson: students map and identify summer crops and water the garden; no hands-on cooking. The closing tasting is an eat-only snack with a sweet/sour thumbs check, not a recorded structured sensory comparison, so the array is empty.

title: Food of Peru
rationale: Students prepare arroz chaufa (Peruvian fried rice): cut vegetables → Knife skills; stir-fry in pans → Sautéing & stir-frying; graters → Grating; mixing bowls → Mixing & stirring; from recipes with measuring → Measuring, Reading & following recipes, Assembling. Rice is pre-cooked, chicha morada is boiled before class, and locro's potatoes/squash are par-cooked/roasted by the teacher in prep, so those don't earn student techniques.

title: Foods From Around the World: China
rationale: Students make smashed cucumber salad: smash cucumbers in a bag with a rolling pin → Mashing; cut cucumbers → Knife skills; grate garlic → Grating; mix a dressing in a jar → Creating sauces & dressings, Mixing & stirring; measuring spoons → Measuring; combine into the salad → Assembling, from a recipe copy → Reading & following recipes. This is a no-heat dish, so no stovetop techniques; tasting is an eat-only snack.

title: Expert’s Guide to Gardening: Compost
rationale: A garden lesson: students observe the worm bin/compost and complete a workbook page; no hands-on cooking. The closing seasonal 'tasting' is an eat-only snack, so the array is empty.
